text.title: Vision Application Rules
text.summary: Token budget, image prep, JSON mode gotchas, and async batch patterns for production vision tasks.

rule.statement: Validate image size and format before base64 encoding. Reject files over 20MB and unsupported formats early.
rule.rationale: OpenAI Vision API rejects images over 20MB. Base64 encoding increases payload size by ~33%; a 20MB image becomes ~27MB on the wire.

rule.statement: Use detail="auto" by default; switch to detail="high" only for dense text documents or charts with small labels.
rule.rationale: detail="high" costs 85 tokens per 512x512 tile plus a base 85 tokens — a 4K image consumes 1500+ prompt tokens just for the image.

example.code:
from PIL import Image import io, base64
def prepare_image_for_api(path: str, max_short_side: int = 768) -> str: """Resize and encode. Keeps detail:auto within token budget.""" img = Image.open(path) w, h = img.size short = min(w, h) if short > max_short_side: scale = max_short_side / short img = img.resize((int(w * scale), int(h * scale)), Image.LANCZOS) buf = io.BytesIO() fmt = "JPEG" if path.lower().endswith((".jpg", ".jpeg")) else "PNG" img.save(buf, format=fmt, quality=85) return base64.standard_b64encode(buf.getvalue()).decode()

rule.statement: Always wrap json.loads() in try/except json.JSONDecodeError when using json_mode=True.
rule.rationale: Vision models hallucinate invalid JSON on complex nested structures. Silent failures propagate to downstream routing.

rule.statement: Define the exact JSON schema in the prompt and use response_format={"type": "json_object"}. json_mode does not guarantee schema adherence — normalize output fields (e.g., enforce lowercase severity values).
rule.rationale: A ContentModerator returning {"severity": "HIGH"} instead of "high" breaks downstream logic silently.

antipattern.description: Using json_mode without a schema in the prompt.
antipattern.reason: Model chooses field names and nesting arbitrarily — output is unpredictable across requests.

rule.statement: For batch processing, use asyncio.gather() with a concurrency limit of 10-20 simultaneous requests.
rule.rationale: Sequential batch_analyze() is 10-100x slower than parallel calls. Rate limits apply per minute; 10-20 concurrent is safe for most tier-2 keys.

rule.statement: Cache base64-encoded images by content hash to avoid re-encoding on retry.
rule.rationale: Re-encoding on each retry wastes CPU and adds latency to the critical path.

rule.statement: For content moderation: set a confidence threshold and route low-confidence results to human review, not to auto-action.
rule.rationale: Vision LLM moderation is probabilistic — false negative rate on subtly harmful content is non-zero. Human review for confidence < 0.7 prevents policy violations.
